The initial observation showed position = initial_position + velocity * time (0 + 5*2 = 10). The first action increased velocity from 5 to 10, and the resulting position is 20. To confirm the relationship is robust and not coincidental, I will test the effect of changing the 'time' variable while keeping initial_position and velocity constant. I will increase time to 3.0 and check if position becomes 0 + 10*3 = 30.

The observations consistently support the equation position = initial_position + velocity * time. Step 0 showed 0 + 5*2 = 10. Step 1 showed 0 + 10*2 = 20. Step 2 (current) shows 0 + 10*3 = 30.
